title: Customers Above Average Spending
description: Find customers whose total spending is greater than the average total spending of all customers who have placed at least one order.
sql_solution: WITH totals AS (SELECT customer_id, SUM(amount) total FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id), avg_val AS (SELECT AVG(total) avg_total FROM totals) SELECT c.name, t.total FROM totals t JOIN customers c ON t.customer_id = c.id WHERE t.total > (SELECT avg_total FROM avg_val);
pyspark_solution:
from pyspark.sql.functions import *

customer_totals = orders.groupBy("customer_id").agg(sum("amount").alias("total"))
average_total = customer_totals.agg(avg("total").alias("avg_total"))

result = (
    customer_totals.alias("t")
    .join(customers.alias("c"), col("t.customer_id") == col("c.id"), "inner")
    .crossJoin(average_total)
    .filter(col("t.total") > col("avg_total"))
    .select(col("c.name").alias("name"), col("t.total").alias("total"))
)
